title: Marie Seymour Lucas

infobox.value: (born **Marie Elizabeth Cornelissen**; 23 April 1850 – 25 November 1921)[[1]](#cite_note-MutualArt-1) was a French-born English painter. She studied in London, where she married painter [John Seymour Lucas](/wiki/John_Seymour_Lucas "John Seymour Lucas"). She lived in England for the rest of her life.
Nichols, K. L. ["Women's Art at the World's Columbian Fair & Exposition, Chicago 1893"](http://arcadiasystems.org/academia/cassatt9c.html#lucas). Retrieved 29 July 2018.

[*Women painters of the world, from the time of Caterina Vigri, 1413-1463, to Rosa Bonheur and the present day*](http://www.gutenberg.org/files/39000/39000-h/39000-h.htm), by Walter Shaw Sparrow, The Art and Life Library, Hodder & Stoughton, 27 Paternoster Row, London, 1905.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English painter

| Marie Seymour Lucas | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | Marie Elizabeth Cornelissen  (1850-04-23)23 April 1850  [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), France |
| Died | 25 November 1921(1921-11-25) (aged 71)  [Hendon](/wiki/Hendon "Hendon"), [Middlesex](/wiki/Middlesex "Middlesex"), England |
| Education | The Royal Academy |
| Known for | [Painting](/wiki/Painting "Painting") |
| Spouse | [John Seymour Lucas](/wiki/John_Seymour_Lucas "John Seymour Lucas")  ​ (m. 1877)​ |

**Marie Seymour Lucas** (born **Marie Elizabeth Cornelissen**; 23 April 1850 – 25 November 1921)[[1]](#cite_note-MutualArt-1) was a French-born English painter. She studied in London, where she married painter [John Seymour Lucas](/wiki/John_Seymour_Lucas "John Seymour Lucas"). She lived in England for the rest of her life.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/Marie_Seymour_Lucas_-_On_the_Threshold.jpg/250px-Marie_Seymour_Lucas_-_On_the_Threshold.jpg)](/wiki/File:Marie_Seymour_Lucas_-_On_the_Threshold.jpg)

*On the Threshold*

Daughter of Louis Dieudonné Cornelissen and Marianne, née Bath, she was born in France.[[2]](#cite_note-ODNB-2) Her parents sent her to England for some of her education, and she attended [St. Martin's Lane Academy](/wiki/St._Martin%27s_Lane_Academy "St. Martin's Lane Academy") and later the [Royal Academy](/wiki/Royal_Academy "Royal Academy"). There she began showing her work in 1877.[[2]](#cite_note-ODNB-2) As she married the painter [John Seymour Lucas](/wiki/John_Seymour_Lucas "John Seymour Lucas") the same year, Marie Cornelissen became known professionally only by her married name. She is known for historical works and genre scenes, but she later concentrated on domestic scenes with children.[[2]](#cite_note-ODNB-2)

Lucas exhibited her work at the [Palace of Fine Arts](/wiki/Museum_of_Science_and_Industry_(Chicago) "Museum of Science and Industry (Chicago)") at the 1893 [World's Columbian Exposition](/wiki/World%27s_Columbian_Exposition "World's Columbian Exposition") in Chicago, Illinois.[[3]](#cite_note-Nichols-3) Her painting of orphans *We are but little children weak, nor born to any high estate* was included in the 1905 book *[Women Painters of the World](/wiki/Women_Painters_of_the_World "Women Painters of the World")*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

She died in Hendon, Middlesex.[[2]](#cite_note-ODNB-2)
